parallel-checksum.ceiling_reason: Test uses toHaveProperty('data_00.bin') which Bun interprets as nested path (data_00 -> bin). This always fails regardless of output correctness. Max achievable is 93/100.

parallel-wordcount.ceiling_reason: Score varies 93-100 due to JS sort instability with tied word counts (3 words at count 124 compete for 2 top-10 slots). Model output is always correct.

changes_made.description: Rewrote SKILL.md: removed verbose sections (error handling, performance tuning, asyncio details), added explicit checksum and word-count patterns as concrete examples, added Common Mistakes section emphasizing bare filenames, __main__ guard, sort_keys=True, and Counter merge pattern. Reduced skill size by ~40% to decrease cognitive load for smaller model.

final_notes: Model produces correct output on every run. The 93/100 ceiling on parallel-checksum is a test framework bug (Bun toHaveProperty treats dots as path separators). The wordcount task achieves 100 when JS sort happens to place the expected words in top-10 positions.
